content:
- Completed the authoritative managed-config harness/docs task end-to-end and pushed commit 3c52b3b.
- All required gates passed in the final state: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint. I also ran make docs-build and make docs-hygiene as planned.
- I created .ralph/tasks/bugs/failsafe-integrity-ultra-long-flake.md because the first full make test-long run flaked in the no-quorum fencing integrity scenario before a clean isolated rerun and a clean full rerun.

- Read the Ralph progress log for Choose-Task, then inspected .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md to apply the mandatory task-selection rules.
- Found exactly one active failing bug task: .ralph/tasks/bugs/failsafe-integrity-ultra-long-flake.md. Confirmed its task file has <passes>false</passes> and no <blocked_by> tags, so it outranks all non-bug, meta, and already-passing tasks.
- Next step was to write that exact task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and quit immediately per the chooser instructions.

- Read prior progress log entry to recover context.
- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and found the active task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/failsafe-integrity-ultra-long-flake.md.
- Next I am opening the task file to detect whether it is at planning, TO BE VERIFIED, or NOW EXECUTE state so I can follow the workflow exactly.

- Inspected failure summary, current passing rerun artifacts, and the multi-node HA helpers.
- Strong finding: the no-quorum fencing scenario currently verifies integrity against any reachable node while quorum remains lost; a passing artifact even accepted row_count=14 while committed_writes=16, so the assertion is weak and race-prone.
- I am now checking whether the harness can restore etcd quorum for a stronger post-recovery verification, and then I will write the detailed plan into the task file with TO BE VERIFIED.

- Read the HA e2e etcd harness files relevant to no-quorum recovery: src/test_harness/etcd3.rs, src/test_harness/ha_e2e/startup.rs, src/test_harness/ha_e2e/handle.rs, and tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs.
- Confirmed that EtcdClusterHandle::shutdown_member() swap_removes the EtcdHandle from the live members vector and only returns bool, so the stopped member handle/spec is discarded from the cluster state.
- I am checking where startup constructs EtcdClusterMemberSpec and whether any higher-level handle preserves those specs or port assignments for later respawn; likely answer is no in-place restart support today.

- Collected exact line references for the etcd harness answer.
- Startup constructs full EtcdClusterSpec/EtcdClusterMemberSpec values, then only stores an EtcdClusterHandle containing live EtcdHandle children plus client endpoint strings.
- Minimal restoration design appears to be: preserve original cluster/member specs somewhere durable (likely on EtcdClusterHandle, possibly surfaced through TestClusterHandle/ClusterFixture) and add a respawn path that can recreate a stopped member with its original name/data_dir/log_dir/client_port/peer_port and cluster-wide initial-cluster context.

- Conclusion for current question: there is no in-place etcd member restart support in the HA e2e harness today.
- EtcdClusterHandle only keeps live EtcdHandle children and client endpoint strings; EtcdHandle itself lacks peer_port/log_dir/etcd_bin/startup_timeout, and shutdown_member() discards the removed handle.
- Minimal viable restoration design is to teach EtcdClusterHandle to retain the original EtcdClusterSpec or equivalent per-member spec map plus cluster-wide spawn params, then expose a restart_member/respawn_member path used through TestClusterHandle/ClusterFixture, which already hold the EtcdClusterHandle.

- Read tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs and traced e2e_no_quorum_fencing_blocks_post_cutoff_commits_and_preserves_integrity.
- Confirmed the scenario records committed_writes and committed_keys, but the final integrity helper only checks COUNT(*) >= min_rows and duplicate PK absence on one reachable node; it does not assert exact presence of the committed keys after recovery.
- Next I am recording this as a bug task via the add-bug skill, then I will summarize the gap and the stronger assertion that matches the intended scenario.

- Added bug task .ralph/tasks/bugs/no-quorum-integrity-check-does-not-verify-committed-keys.md to capture the weak post-scenario assertion.
- The concrete issue is that assert_no_split_brain_write_evidence only checks in-memory uniqueness/hard-failure counters, while assert_table_key_integrity_strict only checks row_count >= min_rows and no duplicate PKs on one reachable node.
- Final response will explain that the best stronger assertion is an exact post-recovery comparison between workload.committed_keys and the recovered table key set on the recovered writable primary, ideally after quorum restoration plus a proof write.

- Confirmed the likely root issue is the scenario design, not a deterministic data corruption bug: the test currently verifies against any reachable node while quorum is still lost.
- A passing artifact proved the assertion is weak and timing-sensitive because it accepted node-2 with row_count=14 while workload.committed_writes=16.
- Explorer confirmed there is no in-place etcd restart support today; the minimal recovery path is to make EtcdClusterHandle retain enough cluster/member spec to respawn stopped members for a post-recovery integrity check.
- Next I am writing the task-file plan with recovery-first verification and exact committed-key matching, then I will stop at TO BE VERIFIED.

- Wrote the detailed draft implementation plan into .ralph/tasks/bugs/failsafe-integrity-ultra-long-flake.md.
- The plan ends with TO BE VERIFIED, per the required workflow, and focuses on recovery-first verification plus exact committed-key matching instead of outage-state loose SQL checks.
- Stopping immediately now so the next pass can do the mandatory skeptical review and alter at least one concrete part of the plan.

- Resumed by reading prior progress and the active Ralph task pointer.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/failsafe-integrity-ultra-long-flake.md and that the worktree already has the drafted plan plus other Ralph bookkeeping changes.
- Next I am reading the task file to determine whether this turn must perform the skeptical TO BE VERIFIED review or move straight to execution.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it ends in TO BE VERIFIED, so this pass must be the skeptical plan review.
- The draft plan currently assumes adding etcd restart support plus exact committed-key verification after recovery.
- Next I am re-checking the harness and scenario code to force at least one concrete plan change before switching the task file marker to NOW EXECUTE.

- Skeptical pass found one concrete plan change already: the scenario should reuse the existing stop_etcd_majority_and_wait_failsafe_strict_all_nodes helper instead of maintaining a manual stop/sample/sleep outage path.
- I am validating that helper now so I can update the task plan with a narrower, less duplicative execution path before switching to NOW EXECUTE.
- I still need one more check on where restart support should live, but the plan will definitely change from the previous draft.

- Completed the skeptical review pass on failsafe-integrity-ultra-long-flake.md and changed the plan concretely before switching the marker to NOW EXECUTE.
- Concrete changes: use stop_etcd_majority_and_wait_failsafe_strict_all_nodes for outage gating, keep restart metadata on EtcdClusterHandle itself, and split exact-key verification into a pure comparison helper plus a SQL wrapper.
- I am now executing the plan starting with etcd harness restart support, then the scenario rewrite, then the full required gates.

- New request is a narrow inspection only: identify what must change for etcd member restart support beyond src/test_harness/etcd3.rs.
- I read prior progress and confirmed the likely hotspot set is tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs plus src/test_harness/ha_e2e/startup.rs, handle.rs, ops.rs, util.rs.
- Next I am tracing ownership boundaries and cleanup/shutdown flows so the answer is concrete about what non-etcd3 code paths need changes and which likely do not.

- Started a docs-only inspection for no-quorum fencing semantics.
- Read prior progress, then searched the tree for no-quorum/fencing/outage-time/recovery-time/exact-key verification references.
- Search points to docs in contributors/testing-system and verification/task-33 as likely stale-if-changed candidates; next I am opening only those specific docs plus the scenario helper for semantic grounding.

- Opened the scenario implementation and confirmed current semantics are outage-time oriented: stop etcd majority, wait/sleep in fail-safe, stop workload, enforce post-cutoff commit tolerance, then run assert_table_key_integrity_strict before any quorum restoration.
- Tried opening docs/src/testing paths cited by the historical verification report, but those pages do not exist at those exact paths in the current tree.
- Next I am resolving the current docs locations that describe HA e2e coverage so I can separate currently stale files from historical artifacts whose references are already path-stale.

- Finished the docs inspection for no-quorum fencing scenario semantics without editing files.
- Confirmed current code semantics in tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs:2890-2983 are outage-time oriented (stop etcd majority, sample fail-safe window, enforce post-cutoff commit tolerance, then strict key-integrity check before quorum restoration).
- The only source-doc paths in the current tree that mention this scenario closely enough to risk semantic staleness are docs/src/contributors/testing-system.md (broad summary wording) and docs/src/verification/task-33-docs-verification-report.md (historical artifact that explicitly references the no-quorum stress scenario and already cites moved/deleted docs paths).
- Next action, if asked, would be to update those docs and regenerate the matching docs/book HTML outputs.

- Traced the ownership boundary for etcd restart support. TestClusterHandle and StartupGuard only own Option<EtcdClusterHandle>; they do not retain separate etcd specs.
- Strong conclusion: if restart metadata lives inside EtcdClusterHandle, ha_e2e startup/handle/ops mostly do not need direct edits; the concrete non-etcd3 changes are mainly in tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs where quorum is intentionally lost and later needs restoration.
- Final answer will call out the exact multi_node helper/scenario lines and explain that startup.rs/ops.rs only need changes if ownership is pushed outward instead of keeping restart state inside EtcdClusterHandle.

- Implemented the first etcd harness patch: EtcdClusterHandle now retains restart metadata, tracks stopped members, and has restart_member/restart_members APIs plus a real-binary restart test.
- I am checking error-conversion details and then I will wire the new restart path into the HA multi-node fixture and exact-key recovery assertions.
- Docs scout already identified docs/src/contributors/testing-system.md and docs/src/verification/task-33-docs-verification-report.md as the main stale-doc candidates for the later doc patch.

- The new etcd restart harness test passed, the pure exact-key comparison tests passed, and the no-quorum fencing integration scenario also passed after the recovery-phase rewrite.
- The integration run showed node-1 remaining fenced/unreachable for a while after quorum return, but the scenario recovered cleanly and completed with exact-key verification after SQL became healthy again.
- Next I am updating the stale docs to describe recovery-time exact-key verification and then I will run the full required gates: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.

- Full make test passed, but make test-long failed specifically on the rewritten no-quorum fencing scenario.
- The failure is informative rather than flaky: recovered primary node-2 had 18 rows while workload.committed_writes was 20, so exact preserved-key equality is not a stable invariant under the full-suite recovery path.
- Next I am inspecting the failing summary/timeline to determine a stronger valid recovery invariant, likely centered on recovered-primary subset/convergence proof rather than exact equality with all client-observed commits.

- Final verification is complete: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint all passed in the final tree.
- The key correction after the first ultra-long failure was to replace the over-strong exact-equality recovery assertion with a bounded recovered-primary invariant: all pre-cutoff committed keys must survive and no recovered key may be outside the client-observed set.
- Next I am updating the Ralph task file checkboxes and pass marker, then I will run task_switch, commit all changes, push, and stop.
